title: psorospermum atrorufum
intro: Psorospermum atrorufum, also known as the bitter leaf or bitter wood, is a plant species in the family Guttiferae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. In herbal preparations, Psorospermum atrorufum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in some parts of the world.
